behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: summary-write path must query stored per-dimension findings for that round before allowing PASS. Unit is too low because the bug appears when persistence/read + validation are wired together. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it only references "stored dimension finding" and "same round" checks, with no real OS/network/subprocess, no model-quality dependency, and no multi-agent sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary writer + findings repository (test DB/in-memory persistence with real repository code). Seed round findings containing at least one FAIL, attempt to store PASS summary, assert rejection and no PASS persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is cross-module contract mismatch: gate logic must consume repository findings, not summary text. Unit-only logic tests can miss wiring where gate is fed wrong source. Higher levels rejected because behavior is about data source selection in local components; no required real HTTP/subprocess/OS and no dependence on non-deterministic model output.
behaviors.test_description: Set stored findings with a FAIL and conflicting manual summary text claiming all PASS. Run stop-gate computation through production wiring (gate service + findings store + summary store) and assert verdict is non-PASS based on stored findings.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans at least validation + audit-record emission/persistence. A unit test of flag parsing alone would miss missing/failed audit write. Higher levels were considered and rejected: text requires "explicit override flag" and "auditable warning record" but does not require real OS/network/subprocess, real LLM behavior, or chained agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise summary submission endpoint/service with and without override flag. Assert: without flag override is rejected; with flag accepted and warning audit record is persisted with actor, round, and reason fields.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the review loop cannot finalize PASS unless a fresh review-agent round is executed on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is orchestration state integrity: finalize step must verify a newer review round tied to updated diff exists. This can be caught with deterministic round metadata and timestamps/hash linkage across modules. Agentic/Workflow were considered but rejected by behavior text: it requires that a "fresh review-agent round is executed" (execution evidence), not that model output quality be evaluated; no need for non-deterministic LLM assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate Phase 4 fix producing a new diff version. Attempt finalize using only pre-fix review round and assert PASS is blocked. Then record a new review round referencing updated diff and assert finalize can proceed (subject to findings).

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings, the flow emits an escalation outcome instead of PASS.
behaviors.justification: This is a state-machine/policy decision across round counter + findings status + outcome emitter. Needs module wiring to reproduce the real bug class. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is deterministic policy ("rounds are exhausted" + "unresolved MUST-FIX"), not real OS/network/subprocess behavior, not model-quality dependent, and not inherently requiring multi-agent non-deterministic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run loop controller with max-rounds reached and unresolved MUST-FIX findings in store. Assert terminal outcome is ESCALATION and PASS is never emitted.
